title: ERP-Lite Operational Atlas
subtitle: A business-facing map of how ERP-lite connects daily selling, customers, stock, payments, reporting, and recovery.

modules.name: Sales Register
modules.tagline: Fast checkout with the business record built in.
modules.description: The register is where staff add items, attach a customer when useful, take payment, and complete a sale without leaving the selling flow.
modules.whyItMatters: A focused register keeps lines moving while still creating the records the business needs later for reporting, support, and customer follow-up.
businessOutcomes: Better customer context without slowing the cashier
modules.merchantConversation: When a cashier rings up a regular, the system can keep the line moving and still remember the visit for future service.
futureExpansion: Deeper order handoff to kitchen or fulfillment screens

modules.name: Customer/CRM
modules.tagline: A lightweight customer memory for everyday service.
modules.description: Customer/CRM links purchases to a customer profile when the shopper shares a phone number or identity at checkout.
modules.whyItMatters: Staff can recognize returning customers and the business can understand repeat visits without turning checkout into a back-office CRM session.
modules.merchantConversation: If a customer asks what they bought last time, the team can answer without digging through paper receipts.

modules.name: Inventory
modules.tagline: Know what can be sold and what needs attention.
modules.description: Inventory tracks physical goods and stock-sensitive products while allowing everyday prepared items to flow through the register normally.
modules.whyItMatters: Operators can protect retail stock from overselling while keeping prepared food and drink sales simple.
businessOutcomes: Separate prepared items from tracked goods
modules.merchantConversation: The register can block a sold-out mug without blocking a made-to-order coffee.

modules.name: Payments & Recovery
modules.tagline: Take payment and keep a clear path when something interrupts.
modules.description: Payments & Recovery manages cash and card sale completion, declined attempts, uncertain payment states, and operator recovery steps.
modules.whyItMatters: Payment interruptions are stressful. The system should show the next safe action instead of leaving the register stuck.
replacesOrReduces: Guessing whether a payment went through
modules.merchantConversation: If a card terminal times out, the cashier gets a recovery path instead of starting over and risking a double charge.

modules.name: Reporting
modules.tagline: Daily numbers that match how the business actually runs.
modules.description: Reporting summarizes sales, taxes, refunds, corrections, tender activity, and customer activity for operators and owners.
modules.whyItMatters: Owners need a clean daily view without rebuilding totals from receipts, payment portals, and spreadsheets.
modules.merchantConversation: At close, the owner can see gross sales, refunds, tax, and payment mix without reconstructing the day by hand.

modules.name: Business Operations
modules.tagline: Manager controls for the parts of the day that need structure.
modules.description: Business Operations covers business-day status, saved tickets, manager attention, operator roles, and safe closeout behavior.
modules.whyItMatters: The day should close only when unresolved work is visible and understood, not silently discarded.
modules.merchantConversation: A saved order stays in the queue with the customer's name and total until someone completes it.

modules.name: AI Assistant
modules.tagline: Operational help without taking over the register.
modules.description: The AI Assistant can help staff find items, understand recovery steps, answer operational questions, and recommend follow-up actions.
modules.whyItMatters: New or busy staff can get guidance without leaving the POS flow or calling a manager for every small question.
modules.merchantConversation: A cashier can ask how to handle a recovery state and get plain-language guidance at the point of need.

modules.name: Content Studio / Social Observer
modules.tagline: Turn real business activity into safer marketing opportunities.
modules.description: Content Studio / Social Observer helps merchants observe operational signals, identify content opportunities, and draft merchant-approved marketing ideas based on what is actually happening in the business.
modules.whyItMatters: Most merchants know they should post more consistently, but they do not have time to invent content from scratch. AtlasADE can turn sales trends, customer patterns, services, products, and seasonal signals into practical content ideas while keeping the merchant in control.
businessOutcomes: Better content ideas from real activity
Campaigns tied to products and services
modules.merchantConversation: Instead of asking "what should I post today?", the merchant can see which products, services, customer patterns, or seasonal moments create a real reason to communicate.

modules.name: Integrations
modules.tagline: Connect the POS to the tools around the business.
modules.description: Integrations are the endpoints ERP-lite can connect to around payments, accounting, inventory, marketing, hardware, and analytics.
modules.whyItMatters: A merchant should not have to re-enter the same business facts into every tool they use.
modules.merchantConversation: The same completed sale can support the receipt, payment record, customer history, and daily report.

modules.name: Backup & Replay
modules.tagline: Protect the business record when devices restart or recover.
modules.description: Backup & Replay protects operational records by preserving the sequence of important business events and allowing recovery after restart.
modules.whyItMatters: A register should not lose or duplicate a sale because a device restarts, power blips, or the system needs to recover.
modules.merchantConversation: If the register restarts, the business record should come back consistently instead of doubling purchase history or reopening closed work.

steps.name: Customer lookup
steps.detail: A customer can be found by phone when they choose to share it.

steps.name: Ticket creation
steps.detail: A clean ticket starts the sale and keeps the cart organized.

steps.name: Items added
steps.detail: The cashier taps menu items or retail goods into the ticket.

steps.name: Inventory/tax checked
steps.detail: The system applies tax rules and checks stock where stock tracking matters.

steps.name: Payment taken
steps.detail: Cash or card payment completes the sale, with recovery guidance if something interrupts.

steps.name: Receipt created
steps.detail: The sale can produce a customer receipt and an auditable business record.

steps.name: Customer timeline updated
steps.detail: When linked, the purchase becomes part of the customer's history.

steps.name: Reports updated
steps.detail: Sales, tax, tender, refund, and closeout summaries reflect the completed work.

steps.name: Backup/replay protects the record
steps.detail: Recovery systems protect the record from restart and replay issues.

steps.name: AI can recommend next actions
steps.detail: The assistant can suggest follow-up, explain an operation, or help staff recover.
